name: Jerry Gonzalez
abstract: Jerry González (June 5, 1949 – October 1, 2018) was an American bandleader, trumpeter and percussionist of Puerto Rican descent. Together with his brother, bassist Andy González, Jerry Gonzalez played an important role in the development of Latin Jazz during the late 20th century.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Latin Roots
Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Latin Roots

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: through my friend who was Jerry Gonzalez, the conga and trumpet player who was a great friend of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ignacio Berroa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: The leader was a vibraphone player, the percussionist was Jerry Gonzalez of Fort Apache fame and his brother Andy Gonzalez. It was at the Music and Art High School in New York. And so I was at that band
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: my dear friend Jerry Gonzalez showed up with a car... and Jerry was my translator.

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: He recommended that I check out a lot of ’50s Tito Puente and also the music of Jerry Gonzalez and the Fort Apache Band
